rubric_identity.one_sentence_purpose: Evaluate CVs for delivery-led AI engineering manager or player-coach roles that prioritize shipped AI platform capabilities, architecture proof, evaluation and governance, and measurable adoption over pure research depth or executive positioning.
rubric_identity.core_persona: Player-coach AI engineering leader who ships and scales AI platform or product capabilities, translates between stakeholders and engineering, and proves quality, governance, and adoption without overstating formal product-management or org-ownership claims.
job_overlay_notes: Manager scope appears in 45.2% of roles while executive scope appears in only 3.2% — frame leadership as delivery ownership not org hierarchy
Platform design appears in 54.8% of roles — architecture proof is a major separator from pure people-managers
GDPR appears in 32.3% of EEA roles — compliance evidence matters when tied to delivery, not as standalone legal expertise
Research-heavy and PhD requirements are 0% — do not reward academic positioning
Title distribution is noisy with Product Manager AI appearing frequently — rely on role-scope evidence not title matching

what_good_looks_like: AI platform and GenAI keywords appear 2-3 times across headline, profile, experience, and skills sections
Platform design and architecture terminology repeated with outcome linkage not just tool lists
Evaluation, quality, guardrails, and governance terms present when evidenced
Stakeholder management and adoption language appears in profile and experience
Keywords match delivery-led manager vocabulary: shipped, scaled, platform, architecture, evaluation, adoption
common_failures: Over-indexing on PM-heavy tool keywords like Airtable, Asana, HubSpot, Notion without actual usage evidence
Using research or academic vocabulary when category requires 0% PhD/research signals
Listing ML frameworks like PyTorch, TensorFlow without production deployment context
Generic AI strategy language without shipped outcome proof
Prompt engineering branding when signal is 0% in category

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Match exact platform terminology from JD (GenAI platform, AI workflow, ML platform) in headline and profile
Mirror evaluation and quality language if JD emphasizes trust and reliability
Add GDPR or privacy keywords only if JD explicitly mentions EEA compliance requirements
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise SaaS roles may weight integration and multi-tenant architecture keywords higher
Startup roles may favor greenfield and 0-to-1 terminology over optimization language
region_overrides: EEA-specific roles should surface GDPR, TCF, or privacy-aware delivery keywords when evidenced
Remote-first keyword adds value given 54.8% remote-first signal but should not dominate

9_10: AI platform, GenAI, or equivalent appears 3+ times with natural distribution across sections
Architecture and platform design terminology linked to measurable outcomes
Evaluation, governance, and adoption keywords all present with evidence backing
Zero PM-heavy tool pollution or research vocabulary that conflicts with category signals
7_8: Core AI platform and architecture keywords present 2+ times
Most category table-stakes terms covered with reasonable distribution
Minor keyword gaps in differentiator areas like evaluation or governance
Occasional generic AI language without creating false impression
5_6: AI platform keywords present but under-distributed across sections
Missing architecture or platform design terminology despite category weight
Some PM-tool or research vocabulary pollution that may confuse ATS matching
Stakeholder and adoption language absent or buried in bullets
0_4: Core AI platform and GenAI keywords absent or appear only once
Dominated by PM-tool keywords like Airtable, Asana without actual evidence
Research or academic vocabulary conflicts with 0% category signal
Generic technology keywords without AI platform specificity

red_flags: Prompt engineering listed as primary skill when category signal is 0%
Heavy ML framework listing (PyTorch, TensorFlow) without production context when each is only 3.2%
PM operations tool keywords dominating when they appear due to title noise not actual category fit
AI evangelist or strategic AI leadership language without delivery proof

what_good_looks_like: Measurable adoption metrics for AI platform delivery: active users, usage growth, workflow volume
Architecture outcomes quantified: latency improvement, reliability gains, integration count
Quality and evaluation metrics present: MRR@k, NDCG@k, defect reduction, incident reduction
Business value outcomes stated: revenue influence, productivity gain, cost avoided
STAR or ARIS format with shipped capability as situation, delivery action, measurable result
common_failures: Activity descriptions without outcome quantification
Architecture work described as technology choices without production impact
Evaluation mentioned without quality score improvements or adoption metrics
Generic team leadership claims without delivery or scale outcomes
User count stated without adoption rate, retention, or efficiency context

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes scale, foreground user counts, throughput, and volume metrics
If JD emphasizes quality, foreground evaluation scores, incident reduction, reliability metrics
If JD emphasizes adoption, foreground growth percentages, time-to-value, workflow efficiency gains
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise SaaS roles value multi-tenant scale metrics and integration counts
Startup roles value 0-to-1 launch velocity and early adoption proof
Platform team roles value developer adoption and plugin ecosystem metrics
region_overrides: EEA roles may weight compliance-linked outcomes: audit pass rates, GDPR revenue protection
No region-specific metric format requirements identified

9_10: Every major achievement includes specific quantified outcome
AI platform delivery metric present: users, adoption rate, or workflow volume
Architecture outcome quantified: latency, reliability, or efficiency improvement
Quality or evaluation metric present with clear before/after or benchmark comparison
Business value stated in concrete terms: revenue, cost, productivity with numbers
7_8: Most achievements include quantified outcomes
AI platform delivery has at least user count or adoption indicator
Architecture or quality work has some quantification even if not comprehensive
Business value implied through concrete outcomes even if not fully monetized
5_6: Some achievements quantified but key AI platform delivery lacks metrics
Architecture described without production impact quantification
User counts present but adoption rate or efficiency gains absent
Business value claims vague or unsubstantiated
0_4: Achievements predominantly activity-based without outcomes
AI platform work lacks any user, adoption, or scale metrics
No quantification of architecture, quality, or delivery impact
Generic claims of success without evidence

red_flags: Round number metrics that appear fabricated: exactly 50%, 10x improvement without context
Outcomes claimed without clear causal link to candidate's work
Business value metrics that exceed plausible scope for role level
Adoption metrics without user count or volume basis to validate scale claim

what_good_looks_like: AI platform and GenAI delivery evidence matches 17/17 deep analysis delivery signal
Platform design and architecture proof present matching 54.8% platform design prevalence
Stakeholder management and communication evident matching 67.7% and 96.8% soft skill signals
Evaluation and quality mechanisms shown when present in JD matching 22.6% signal
Guardrails and governance evidence when JD requires matching 19.4% signal
common_failures: Pure IC architecture positioning for roles requiring manager scope (45.2%)
Executive positioning for roles that are actually manager or player-coach level
PM ownership claims when JD seeks engineering leadership not product management
Research or experimentation focus when category has 0% research-heavy signal
Missing stakeholder translation proof for roles emphasizing cross-functional alignment

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD emphasizes team leadership, surface mentoring, org-building, or hiring evidence
If JD emphasizes platform architecture, foreground hybrid retrieval, caching, integration work
If JD emphasizes governance, surface guardrails, access control, compliance evidence
If JD mentions specific AI capabilities (agents, RAG, evaluation), match terminology
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise roles may require multi-tenant, integration-heavy evidence
Consumer roles may weight user adoption and engagement metrics higher
B2B SaaS roles may value plugin ecosystem and workflow automation proof
region_overrides: EEA roles should surface GDPR or privacy-aware delivery when JD mentions compliance
Remote-first JDs should reference distributed execution capability
No native language requirements in category — English proficiency sufficient

9_10: All table-stakes signals covered: AI delivery, platform design, stakeholder management, business value
Multiple differentiators present matching JD emphasis: evaluation, governance, roadmap contribution
Role scope matches JD: player-coach framing for manager-level, not executive inflation
Domain and region fit evident through relevant compliance or market context
7_8: Table-stakes signals well covered with minor gaps
At least one differentiator present and well evidenced
Role scope appropriate with no inflation or misalignment
Domain fit reasonable though not perfectly tailored
5_6: Some table-stakes missing: platform design or stakeholder translation absent
Differentiators not surfaced despite JD emphasis
Role scope ambiguous: unclear if manager or IC positioning
Generic positioning that could apply to multiple categories
0_4: Multiple table-stakes missing: no AI delivery or architecture evidence
Positioning conflicts with JD: executive framing for manager role or PM claims for engineering role
Domain or region mismatch: research focus for delivery role
CV appears targeted at different category entirely

red_flags: Product Manager or PM ownership claims when JD seeks engineering leadership
Executive or VP positioning when JD describes manager or player-coach scope
Research or academic emphasis when JD requires shipped production systems
Missing AI platform evidence for role centered on AI delivery

what_good_looks_like: Hands-on team leadership with delivery ownership evident through shipped outcomes
Player-coach positioning: combines technical depth with stakeholder translation
Mentoring and team development evidence without overclaiming formal management authority
Cross-functional alignment proof: engineering, design, leadership, executives as partners
Roadmap contribution framed as translation and prioritization input not ownership
Communication and collaboration evident at 96.8% and 87.1% category signals
common_failures: Inflating to director, VP, or executive ownership when category is 3.2% executive
Claiming formal people management without explicit evidence of direct reports
Pure IC positioning that misses manager scope present in 45.2% of roles
Generic leadership claims without specific delivery or team development outcomes
Formal PM or roadmap ownership claims inappropriate for engineering leadership positioning

what_to_adjust_per_jd: If JD specifies team size, surface relevant mentoring or team-scaling evidence
If JD emphasizes stakeholder management, foreground cross-functional alignment outcomes
If JD mentions hiring, surface recruiting or interview process leadership
If JD is IC-leaning within manager category, emphasize player-coach technical depth
company_domain_overrides: Startup roles may accept smaller team evidence with broader ownership scope
Enterprise roles may require evidence of operating across larger org structures
Platform team roles may weight developer enablement and adoption leadership
region_overrides: Remote-first roles may value distributed team leadership evidence
EEA roles do not require region-specific leadership style adjustments

9_10: Clear player-coach positioning with both technical depth and team leadership
Mentoring evidence with specific outcomes: promotions supported, engineers developed
Stakeholder translation demonstrated through concrete alignment decisions
Delivery ownership evident without inflating to executive scope
Hiring or team-building evidence when claimed matches actual scope
7_8: Player-coach positioning present though technical or leadership side stronger
Some mentoring or team development evidence without full quantification
Stakeholder collaboration evident without specific translation outcomes
Appropriate scope claims without inflation
5_6: Leadership positioning unclear: pure IC or generic manager language
Mentoring mentioned without evidence of outcomes or team development
Stakeholder interaction implied but not demonstrated
Scope claims vague enough to create uncertainty
0_4: Executive or VP positioning inappropriate for manager-level category
Formal management claims without any supporting evidence
Pure IC positioning missing leadership component for manager-scoped role
Generic leadership buzzwords without delivery or team outcomes

red_flags: Unsupported people-management framing: direct report counts, performance reviews, compensation without evidence
Budget or P&L ownership claims when category signal is 6.5%
Head of AI or VP-level positioning for roles where executive scope is 3.2%
Formal product ownership claims inappropriate for engineering leadership category

what_good_looks_like: All metrics traceable to specific projects or role evidence
Title matches actual role held, not aspirational category title
People management claims only when explicit evidence of direct reports exists
PM, roadmap, or product ownership language only when actually held
Tool and technology claims limited to actual usage not category keyword stuffing
No research, publications, or PhD claims when candidate lacks academic credentials
common_failures: Fabricated adoption or scale metrics without evidence basis
Title inflation: Engineering Manager when actual title was Lead Engineer
Claiming direct reports or team size without explicit evidence
PM ownership claims when role was engineering collaboration not product ownership
Tool claims (Airtable, Asana, HubSpot) added for ATS matching without actual usage
Research or publication claims for candidate without academic background

what_to_adjust_per_jd: Verify any compliance claims match actual regulatory work performed
Verify team size claims match JD expectations without inflation
Verify architecture claims match actual system design work not just usage
company_domain_overrides: Enterprise claims should match actual enterprise customer evidence
Startup claims should match actual early-stage company experience
Scale claims should match company size and role scope plausibility
region_overrides: GDPR claims must reflect actual compliance work not just EEA employment
Remote leadership claims should match actual distributed team experience

9_10: Every metric traceable to specific project or company evidence
Title exactly matches role held with no inflation
All people management claims supported by explicit direct report evidence
Tool and technology claims limited to demonstrated actual usage
No unsupported PM, budget, or ownership claims
7_8: Metrics largely traceable with minor ambiguity on one or two items
Title appropriate with at most minor scope clarification needed
People leadership claims supported by mentoring evidence even if formal reports unclear
Tool claims reasonable for role context
5_6: Some metrics lack clear evidence basis
Title may slightly overstate actual role scope
People management implications without explicit evidence
Some tool claims potentially added for ATS matching
0_4: Multiple metrics appear fabricated or lack any evidence basis
Title significantly inflated from actual role
People management claims clearly unsupported
Extensive tool or technology claims inconsistent with background
PM, budget, or ownership claims without evidence

red_flags: Unsupported metrics: round numbers (50%, 10x, 100%) without context or evidence basis
Title fabrication: claiming Engineering Manager when curated evidence shows different title
PhD or publication claims without academic credentials in evidence
Direct report counts without explicit evidence of formal reporting relationships
PM, GTM, or pricing ownership claims when role was engineering collaboration
Budget or P&L claims when category signal is only 6.5% and no evidence exists
Tool claims for Airtable, Asana, HubSpot, Notion added for ATS when not actually used
Research-heavy framing when category has 0% research signal and candidate lacks research background

pass_criteria: AI platform or GenAI delivery evidence present with at least one shipped capability
Platform design or architecture proof present matching 54.8% category signal
Stakeholder management or cross-functional collaboration evidence present matching 67.7% signal
At least one measurable outcome: adoption, quality, delivery, or business value
fail_conditions: No AI platform or GenAI delivery evidence despite category centering on shipped AI value
No architecture or platform design evidence when 54.8% of roles require it
No stakeholder or cross-functional evidence when 67.7% require stakeholder management
No quantified outcomes for any achievement

pass_criteria: No formal PM ownership claims without explicit product management evidence
No people management claims without direct report or formal authority evidence
No budget or P&L claims when category signal is only 6.5%
No research or publication claims without academic credentials
No executive or VP positioning when category executive signal is 3.2%
No tool claims (Airtable, Asana, HubSpot) without actual usage evidence
fail_conditions: PM, GTM, or pricing ownership claimed without explicit evidence
Direct reports or performance management claimed without evidence
Head of AI, VP, or executive positioning inappropriate for manager category
Research-heavy framing when category is 0% research-heavy
Tool claims added for ATS matching without actual usage

pass_criteria: Positioning matches player-coach or delivery-led manager archetype
Technical depth evident alongside leadership and stakeholder capability
Scope claims appropriate for manager level not executive
Tone matches player-coach: shipped, scaled, partnered not strategic visionary
fail_conditions: Positioning as pure IC without leadership for manager-scoped category
Positioning as executive or VP for category with 3.2% executive signal
Positioning as PM or product owner for engineering leadership category
Research or academic positioning for delivery-focused category

scoring_guidance.how_to_interpret_executive_presence: For this manager/player-coach category, executive_presence means hands-on team leadership combined with delivery ownership, not C-suite or boardroom presence. Evaluate for mentoring outcomes, stakeholder translation, cross-functional alignment, and hiring or team development — all tied to shipped AI capabilities. Penalize VP, Head of, or executive positioning since category executive signal is only 3.2%. Player-coach and manager-scope language is safe and expected for 45.2% + 16.1% of roles.
scoring_guidance.how_to_penalize_unsupported_claims: Apply automatic 2-point penalty per unsupported claim in anti_hallucination dimension. Unsupported claims include: fabricated metrics without evidence basis, title inflation beyond actual role, direct report counts without explicit evidence, PM/budget/P&L ownership without evidence, research or publication claims without credentials, and tool claims for ATS matching without actual usage. Gate failure on multiple unsupported claims should cap overall score below good_match threshold.
scoring_guidance.how_to_handle_category_vs_job_tradeoffs: Weight JD-specific signals over generic category signals when they conflict. If JD emphasizes evaluation and governance but candidate evidence is stronger on pure architecture, score jd_alignment lower despite strong architecture. If JD is IC-leaning within the manager category, reduce executive_presence weight for that specific evaluation. Category signals establish baseline expectations; JD signals fine-tune the scoring.

evidence_ledger.rubric_rule: AI platform and GenAI delivery is table-stakes requiring 2-3 keyword occurrences and shipped capability evidence
Platform design and architecture proof required matching 54.8% prevalence in category roles
Stakeholder management evidence required matching 67.7% category signal with communication at 96.8%
Executive presence framed as player-coach leadership not VP or executive since executive scope is only 3.2%
PM, budget, and performance management claims unsafe without explicit evidence given 25.8% hiring and 6.5% budget signals
Research, PhD, and publication claims penalized when category has 0% research-heavy and 0% requires-PhD signals
Evaluation and governance evidence serves as differentiator matching 22.6% and 19.4% category signals respectively
GDPR and privacy compliance evidence valued when tied to delivery given 32.3% EEA prevalence
Tool claims for PM operations software (Airtable, Asana, HubSpot) penalized without actual usage evidence due to title distribution noise
